AZ-104 Scenario-Based Test Book

This scenario-based test book is generated only from the latest AZ-104 source book content. Each chapter contains case studies with shared scenario context and linked MCQs. Each case has 5 linked questions, and each chapter has at least 15 questions total. If a chapter has more extracted facts, additional questions are generated so every fact is covered.

Chapter 1: Module 1.1: Introduction to Azure Cloud Shell

Subtitle: What is Azure Cloud Shell? / How does Azure Cloud Shell work? / Access Cloud Shell

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'What is Azure Cloud Shell? / How does Azure Cloud Shell work? / Access Cloud Shell'.

Current state: On many occasions, you're asked to perform operations on these cloud resources at moments when you’re not using your work laptop.

Operational detail: Azure Cloud Shell is a browser-accessible command-line experience for managing Azure resources.

Constraint: You need to open multiple sessions at the same time. Azure Cloud Shell allows only one instance at time and isn't suitable for concurrent work across multiple subscriptions or tenants.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 1.1: Introduction to Azure Cloud Shell' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'When should you use Azure Cloud Shell?'?

A. Azure Cloud Shell is a command-line environment you can access through your web browser.
B. Use either Bash or PowerShell, whichever you prefer, to manage Azure resources.
C. You can use this environment to manage Azure resources, including VMs, storage, and networking.
D. Rather than having to configure an Azure CLI or PowerShell session from your workstation, you can access Cloud Shell on any standard, compliant browser.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'When should you use Azure Cloud Shell?' in this chapter.
> Use either Bash or PowerShell, whichever you prefer, to manage Azure resources.

Question 2 (Case 1, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'What is Azure Cloud Shell?'?

A. You need to open multiple sessions at the same time. Azure Cloud Shell allows only one instance at time and isn't suitable for concurrent work across multiple subscriptions or tenants.
B. Finally, you can use the Cloud Shell editor to make changes to files, such as scripts, that are saved into this cloud storage directly from the Cloud Shell interface.
C. You need storage from different regions. You might need to back up and synchronize this content since only one region can have the storage allocated to Azure Cloud Shell.
D. Rather than having to configure an Azure CLI or PowerShell session from your workstation, you can access Cloud Shell on any standard, compliant browser.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'What is Azure Cloud Shell?' in this chapter.
> Finally, you can use the Cloud Shell editor to make changes to files, such as scripts, that are saved into this cloud storage directly from the Cloud Shell interface.

Question 3 (Case 1, Q3)
Which response matches the recommended approach in 'When should you use Azure Cloud Shell?'?

A. Rather than having to configure an Azure CLI or PowerShell session from your workstation, you can access Cloud Shell on any standard, compliant browser.
B. You need storage from different regions. You might need to back up and synchronize this content since only one region can have the storage allocated to Azure Cloud Shell.
C. You can use this environment to manage Azure resources, including VMs, storage, and networking.
D. This VM is preconfigured with the latest versions of PowerShell and Bash.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'When should you use Azure Cloud Shell?' in this chapter.
> You need storage from different regions. You might need to back up and synchronize this content since only one region can have the storage allocated to Azure Cloud Shell.

Question 4 (Case 1, Q4)
Which statement best fits the requirement described in 'What is Azure Cloud Shell?'?

A. On many occasions, you're asked to perform operations on these cloud resources at moments when you’re not using your work laptop.
B. You need storage from different regions. You might need to back up and synchronize this content since only one region can have the storage allocated to Azure Cloud Shell.
C. Just like you do when using the Azure CLI or Azure PowerShell.
D. You need to open multiple sessions at the same time. Azure Cloud Shell allows only one instance at time and isn't suitable for concurrent work across multiple subscriptions or tenants.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'What is Azure Cloud Shell?' in this chapter.
> Just like you do when using the Azure CLI or Azure PowerShell.

Question 5 (Case 1, Q5)

A. You need to open multiple sessions at the same time. Azure Cloud Shell allows only one instance at time and isn't suitable for concurrent work across multiple subscriptions or tenants.

C. These applications use different cloud resources, such as Azure VMs, Azure blob storage, Azure networking, and others.

Chapter 2: Module 1.2: Deploy Azure infrastructure by using JSON ARM templates

Subtitle: Explore Azure Resource Manager template structure / What is infrastructure as code? / What is an ARM template?

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'Explore Azure Resource Manager template structure / What is infrastructure as code? / What is an ARM template?'.

Current state: ARM templates allow you to declare what you intend to deploy without having to write the sequence of programming commands to create it.

Operational detail: Now, you're ready to deploy an actual resource.

Constraint: az group create --name <resource-group-name> --location <location>.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 1.2: Deploy Azure infrastructure by using JSON ARM templates' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Deploy an ARM template to Azure'?

A. You can provide these values in a parameter file, by command-line parameters, or in the Azure portal.
B. Both commands require the resource group, the region, and the name for the deployment so you can easily identify it in the deployment history.
C. Update resources to use the storageSKU parameter.
D. In your ARM template's outputs section, you can specify the values that are returned after a successful deployment.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Deploy an ARM template to Azure' in this chapter.
> Both commands require the resource group, the region, and the name for the deployment so you can easily identify it in the deployment history.

Question 2 (Case 1, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Exercise - Add parameters and outputs to your Azure Resource Manager template'?

A. Create a parameter for the storageName value.
B. // This is the allowed values for an Azure storage account
C. Go to your resource group, and see that there are now 3 Succeeded deployments.
D. Then, you add a parameter to define which storage-account SKUs are allowed, and define which one to use for this deployment.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Exercise - Add parameters and outputs to your Azure Resource Manager template' in this chapter.
> Then, you add a parameter to define which storage-account SKUs are allowed, and define which one to use for this deployment.

Question 3 (Case 1, Q3)
Which response matches the recommended approach in 'Create and set the default resource group'?

A. Be sure that Azure PowerShell Tools are installed from the Visual Studio Code Extensions.
B. By setting the default resource group, you can omit that parameter from the Azure CLI commands in this exercise.
C. ARM templates allow you to automate deployments and use the practice of infrastructure as code (IaC).
D. If you have multiple subscriptions, select the subscription you want to use for this exercise.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Create and set the default resource group' in this chapter.
> By setting the default resource group, you can omit that parameter from the Azure CLI commands in this exercise.

Question 4 (Case 1, Q4)
Which statement best fits the requirement described in 'ARM-template parameters'?

A. The top section of the preceding code sets the Azure CLI variables, which include the path to the template file to deploy and the name of the deployment.
B. The storage account name is hardcoded.
C. The bottom section, az deployment group create , deploys the template to Azure.
D. In the parameters section of the template, you specify which values you can input when you deploy the resources.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'ARM-template parameters' in this chapter.
> In the parameters section of the template, you specify which values you can input when you deploy the resources.

Question 5 (Case 1, Q5)
Which action best aligns with the guidance in section 'Add a resource to the ARM template'?

A. The top section of the preceding code sets the Azure CLI variables, which include the path to the template file to deploy and the name of the deployment.
B. Both commands require the resource group, the region, and the name for the deployment so you can easily identify it in the deployment history.
C. The resource location is set to the same location as the resource group where the resource is deployed. Leave the default here.
D. A required section that defines the actual items you want to deploy or update in a resource group or a subscription.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Add a resource to the ARM template' in this chapter.
> The resource location is set to the same location as the resource group where the resource is deployed. Leave the default here.

Question 8 (Case 2, Q3)

B. Update resources to use the storageSKU parameter.
C. Bicep is a language for defining your Azure resources.
D. Recall that ARM templates are idempotent , which means you can deploy the template to the same environment again, and if nothing changes in the template, nothing changes in the environment.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Add another parameter that limits allowed values' in this chapter.
> Update resources to use the storageSKU parameter.

Case Study 3

Scenario: A migration project is underway and the team is validating 'Explore Azure Resource Manager template structure / What is infrastructure as code? / What is an ARM template?'.

Current state: ARM templates are JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) files that define the infrastructure and configuration for your deployment.

Operational detail: Recall that ARM templates are idempotent , which means you can deploy the template to the same environment again, and if nothing changes in the template, nothing changes in the environment.

Constraint: ARM templates allow you to declare what you intend to deploy without having to write the sequence of programming commands to create it.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 1.2: Deploy Azure infrastructure by using JSON ARM templates' guidance.

Question 11 (Case 3, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Deploy the ARM template with an output'?

A. You also add usefulness to the Azure Resource Manager template (ARM template) by adding an output that you can use later in the deployment process.
B. Go to your resource group, and see that there are now 3 Succeeded deployments.
C. Deploy the template by running the following commands. Be sure to replace {your-unique-name} with a string unique to you. templateFile="azuredeploy.json"
D. ARM templates are JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) files that define the infrastructure and configuration for your deployment.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Deploy the ARM template with an output' in this chapter.
> Deploy the template by running the following commands. Be sure to replace {your-unique-name} with a string unique to you. templateFile="azuredeploy.json"

Question 14 (Case 3, Q4)

Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Deploy the template to Azure' in this chapter.

Scenario: During a governance review for 'Explore Azure Resource Manager template structure / What is infrastructure as code? / What is an ARM template?', you must provide a defensible recommendation.

Question 7 (Case 2, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Examine Microsoft Entra ID as a directory service for cloud apps'?

A. It’s important to realize that using Microsoft Entra isn’t the same as deploying an Active Directory domain controller on an Azure virtual machine and adding it to your on-premises domain.
B. Deploying AD DS on an Azure virtual machine requires one or more extra Azure data disks because you shouldn't use drive C for AD DS storage.
C. Microsoft Entra ID is a comprehensive solution for managing identities, enforcing access policies, and securing your applications and data in the cloud and on-premises.
D. In particular, you can enable Microsoft Entra authentication for the Web Apps feature of Azure App Service directly from the Authentication/Authorization blade in the Azure portal.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Examine Microsoft Entra ID as a directory service for cloud apps' in this chapter.
> In particular, you can enable Microsoft Entra authentication for the Web Apps feature of Azure App Service directly from the Authentication/Authorization blade in the Azure portal.

Question 8 (Case 2, Q3)
Which response matches the recommended approach in 'Examine Microsoft Entra Domain Services'?

A. At any given time, an Azure subscription must be associated with one, and only one, Microsoft Entra tenant.
B. Microsoft Entra users and groups are created in a flat structure, and there are no OUs or GPOs.
C. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced IT professional, this module provides you with the knowledge and skills necessary to understand Microsoft Entra ID effectively.
D. By using Microsoft Entra Domain Services, you can freely migrate applications that use LDAP, NTLM, or the Kerberos protocols from your on-premises infrastructure to the cloud.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Examine Microsoft Entra Domain Services' in this chapter.
> By using Microsoft Entra Domain Services, you can freely migrate applications that use LDAP, NTLM, or the Kerberos protocols from your on-premises infrastructure to the cloud.

Question 9 (Case 2, Q4)

A. Each Microsoft Entra tenant is assigned the default Domain Name System (DNS) domain name, consisting of a unique prefix.
B. Conditional Access based on device, group, or location . This lets you configure conditional access for critical resources, based on several criteria.
C. By designating the Microsoft Entra tenant, you can ensure that only users with accounts in that directory can access the website.
D. However, this isn't a significant shortcoming, because OUs in AD DS are used primarily for Group Policy scoping and delegation.

Question 10 (Case 2, Q5)
Which action best aligns with the guidance in section 'Characteristics of Microsoft Entra ID'?

A. In particular, you can enable Microsoft Entra authentication for the Web Apps feature of Azure App Service directly from the Authentication/Authorization blade in the Azure portal.
B. Microsoft Entra ID doesn't use Kerberos authentication; instead, it uses HTTP and HTTPS protocols such as SAML, WS-Federation, and OpenID Connect for authentication, and uses OAuth for authorization.
C. You can enable Microsoft Entra Domain Services for this virtual network so that all on-premises users and services can use domain services from Microsoft Entra ID.
D. Microsoft Entra ID provides developers with centralized authentication and authorization for applications in Azure by using other identity providers or on-premises AD DS.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Characteristics of Microsoft Entra ID' in this chapter.
> Microsoft Entra ID doesn't use Kerberos authentication; instead, it uses HTTP and HTTPS protocols such as SAML, WS-Federation, and OpenID Connect for authentication, and uses OAuth for authorization.

B. Its most elementary form, which any new Azure subscription includes automatically, doesn't incur any extra cost and is referred to as the Free tier.
C. In particular, you can enable Microsoft Entra authentication for the Web Apps feature of Azure App Service directly from the Authentication/Authorization blade in the Azure portal.
D. Because of this, each cloud service that needs authentication will create its own Microsoft Entra tenant.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Characteristics of AD DS' in this chapter.
> Deploying AD DS on an Azure virtual machine requires one or more extra Azure data disks because you shouldn't use drive C for AD DS storage.

Chapter 5: Module 2.3: Describe the core architectural components of Azure

Subtitle: What is Microsoft Azure / What does Azure offer? / What can I do with Azure?

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'What is Microsoft Azure / What does Azure offer? / What can I do with Azure?'.

Current state: In a multi-subscription account, you can configure different billing models and access policies.

Operational detail: You explored how resources and resource groups are related, and how subscriptions and management groups can help manage resources.

Constraint: Azure provides hundreds of services that enable you to do everything from running your existing applications on virtual machines to exploring new software paradigms, such as intelligent bots and generative AI.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 2.3: Describe the core architectural components of Azure' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Use availability zones for your workloads'?

A. A subscription provides access to Azure products and services and serves as a billing unit.
B. Understanding this hierarchy helps you organize resources, control who can access what, and manage costs as your Azure usage grows.
C. Some services or virtual machine (VM) features are only available in certain regions, such as specific VM sizes or storage types.
D. Non-regional services: Services are always available from Azure geographies and are resilient to zone-wide outages as well as region-wide outages.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Use availability zones for your workloads' in this chapter.
> Non-regional services: Services are always available from Azure geographies and are resilient to zone-wide outages as well as region-wide outages.

Question 2 (Case 1, Q2)

C. In Azure, you can host the app on virtual machines or managed app services, store data in managed databases, and monitor health from a centralized dashboard.
D. You can also create a new subscription that enables you to start paying for Azure services you need beyond the limits of a free account.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Additional advantages of region pairs:' in this chapter.
> Planned Azure updates are rolled out to paired regions one region at a time to minimize downtime and risk of application outage.

Case Study 3

Scenario: A migration project is underway and the team is validating 'What is Microsoft Azure / What does Azure offer? / What can I do with Azure?'.

Current state: Billing : Create subscriptions to track costs separately — for instance, one for production workloads and another for development and testing.

Operational detail: Availability zones are physically separate datacenters within an Azure region.

Constraint: Instead, datacenters are grouped into Azure Regions and Azure Availability Zones that provide resiliency and reliability for your workloads.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 2.3: Describe the core architectural components of Azure' guidance.

Question 11 (Case 3, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Additional advantages of region pairs:'?

A. In a multi-subscription account, you can configure different billing models and access policies.
B. When you're working with your own applications and workloads, you create an Azure account, and a subscription is created for you.
C. If you're practicing by creating resources in Azure, monitor usage and remove resources you no longer need to avoid unexpected costs.
D. Most regions are paired in two directions, meaning they are the backup for the region that provides a backup for them (West US and East US back each other up).

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Additional advantages of region pairs:' in this chapter.
> Most regions are paired in two directions, meaning they are the backup for the region that provides a backup for them (West US and East US back each other up).

Question 12 (Case 3, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Azure subscriptions'?

A. These datacenters are facilities with servers arranged in racks, with dedicated power, cooling, and networking infrastructure — similar to an on-premises datacenter, but at a much larger scale.
B. Migrating your existing apps to VMs is a good start, but the cloud is much more than a different place to run your VMs.
C. A subscription provides access to Azure products and services and serves as a billing unit.
D. Azure is a continually expanding set of cloud services that help you meet current and future IT challenges.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Azure subscriptions' in this chapter.
> A subscription provides access to Azure products and services and serves as a billing unit.

Question 13 (Case 3, Q3)
Which response matches the recommended approach in 'Management group, subscriptions, and resource group hierarchy'?

A. Most Azure regions are paired with another region within the same geography (such as US, Europe, or Asia) at least 300 miles away.
B. Anything you create, provision, or deploy is a resource.
C. But when you have many subscriptions across multiple teams or geographies, you need a way to manage access and policies at a higher level.
D. Each management group and subscription can have only one parent.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Management group, subscriptions, and resource group hierarchy' in this chapter.
> Each management group and subscription can have only one parent.

Question 14 (Case 3, Q4)
Which statement best fits the requirement described in 'Region pairs'?

A. Examples of region pairs in Azure are West US paired with East US and Southeast Asia paired with East Asia.
B. VMs, virtual networks, databases, and Azure AI services are all examples of resources.
C. Team and workload boundaries : Give each project its own subscription so costs are easy to track, or separate sandbox environments from production.
D. Azure provides hundreds of services that enable you to do everything from running your existing applications on virtual machines to exploring new software paradigms, such as intelligent bots and generative AI.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Region pairs' in this chapter.
> Examples of region pairs in Azure are West US paired with East US and Southeast Asia paired with East Asia.

Question 15 (Case 3, Q5)
Which action best aligns with the guidance in section 'Practical example'?

A. Most Azure regions are paired with another region within the same geography (such as US, Europe, or Asia) at least 300 miles away.
B. You can purchase Azure access directly from Microsoft by signing up on the Azure website or through a Microsoft representative.
C. As demand increases, you can scale resources up or out and then scale back when demand drops so you're not paying for unused capacity year-round.
D. In Azure, subscriptions are a unit of management, billing, and scale.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Practical example' in this chapter.
> As demand increases, you can scale resources up or out and then scale back when demand drops so you're not paying for unused capacity year-round.

Case Study 4

Scenario: An operations incident affects resources related to 'What is Microsoft Azure / What does Azure offer? / What can I do with Azure?'.

Current state: Non-regional services: Services are always available from Azure geographies and are resilient to zone-wide outages as well as region-wide outages.

Operational detail: These datacenters are facilities with servers arranged in racks, with dedicated power, cooling, and networking infrastructure — similar to an on-premises datacenter, but at a much larger scale.

Constraint: In a one-direction pairing, the Primary region does not provide backup for its secondary region.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 2.3: Describe the core architectural components of Azure' guidance.

> In Azure, subscriptions are a unit of management, billing, and scale.

Current state: If you have many subscriptions, you might need a way to efficiently manage access, policies, and compliance for those subscriptions.

Operational detail: Waiver - The exemption is granted because the noncompliance state of the resource is temporarily accepted.

Constraint: This location determines the scope to which the initiative or policy can be assigned.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 2.4: Azure Policy initiatives' guidance.

Question 96 (Case 20, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Resource compliance states'?

A. Azure built-in policy initiatives are a powerful tool set that enables centralized control across Azure resources and enforcement of specific configurations.
B. The compliance percentage is determined by dividing Compliant , Exempt , and Unknown resources by total resources.
C. A policy or initiative is newly assigned to a scope
D. It allows Azure Policy to modify requests to Azure Resource Manager by altering fields to ensure compliance.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Resource compliance states' in this chapter.
> The compliance percentage is determined by dividing Compliant , Exempt , and Unknown resources by total resources.

Question 97 (Case 20, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Effect types ( then blocks)'?

A. "description": "The list of locations that can be specified when deploying resources.",
B. This block defines the effect that takes place when the policy rule is evaluated to match the condition resources.
C. To bypass the caching delay, you can sign out and sign back in to refresh the Azure Resource Manager cache, which ensures that the new policy is applied immediately to the defined scope.
D. Create assignment - Define deployment rings (1 to 5) by using resource selectors.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Effect types ( then blocks)' in this chapter.
> This block defines the effect that takes place when the policy rule is evaluated to match the condition resources.

Question 98 (Case 20, Q3)
Which response matches the recommended approach in 'Policy enforcement and safe deployment best practices'?

A. The objective when designing an Azure Policy should be to strike a balance between control and stability in one area with speed and results in the other.
B. When assigning policies that include deny or modify actions, beginning with enforcementMode Disabled allows you to view the compliance state and evaluate policy outcomes without triggering actions or denying operations.
C. The first part of the policyRule in an Azure Policy definition is the if block.
D. All capabilities that are available in the portal are also available through PowerShell, Azure CLI, REST APIs, and client SDKs.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Policy enforcement and safe deployment best practices' in this chapter.
> When assigning policies that include deny or modify actions, beginning with enforcementMode Disabled allows you to view the compliance state and evaluate policy outcomes without triggering actions or denying operations.

Question 99 (Case 20, Q4)
Which statement best fits the requirement described in 'Hierarchy for governance'?

A. The deployIfNotExists policy definition runs a template deployment when the condition is met.
B. If you grant or deny access to a resource group, all resources in the resource group are also granted or denied access.
C. A policy definition can contain several conditional statements.
D. Azure Policy is a service that allows you to create, assign, and manage governance policies that enforce rules and effects over Azure resources to ensure that they stay compliant with your IT governance standards.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Hierarchy for governance' in this chapter.
> If you grant or deny access to a resource group, all resources in the resource group are also granted or denied access.

Question 100 (Case 20, Q5)
Which action best aligns with the guidance in section 'Cloud governance with Azure Policy'?

A. The policy effect is enforced during resource creation or update.
B. You can consolidate all compliance data into a singular repository, which simplifies auditing processes for effective cloud compliance and resource governance.
C. The disabled effect is a way to deactivate the policy.
D. Azure Policy allows individual Azure services to implement an Azure Policy extension, enhancing policy behavior and integration with specific resource providers.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Cloud governance with Azure Policy' in this chapter.
> You can consolidate all compliance data into a singular repository, which simplifies auditing processes for effective cloud compliance and resource governance.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Role definition (what)' in this chapter.
> Azure includes several built-in roles that you can use.

Chapter 8: Module 2.6: Allow users to reset their password with Microsoft Entra self-service password reset

Subtitle: What is self-service password reset in Microsoft Entra ID? / Why use SSPR? / How SSPR works

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'What is self-service password reset in Microsoft Entra ID? / Why use SSPR? / How SSPR works'.

Current state: If you created a Premium trial Microsoft Entra tenant for this module, you can delete the tenant 30 days after the trial has expired.

Operational detail: Delete the group you created in Microsoft Entra ID : Go to Microsoft Entra ID > Manage > Groups . Check the box next to the group and select Delete . Select OK .

Constraint: In the Microsoft Entra organization you created, under Manage , select Groups .

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 2.6: Allow users to reset their password with Microsoft Entra self-service password reset' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Enable SSPR'?

A. Users from an existing on-premises domain can use Microsoft Entra Connect, while new users from a merger can use cloud sync in another domain.
B. In your Microsoft Entra organization, under Manage , select Password reset .
C. Tutorial: Enable users to unlock their account or reset passwords using Microsoft Entra self-service password reset
D. You want to understand how you can configure Azure to allow users to manage their own passwords.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Enable SSPR' in this chapter.
> In your Microsoft Entra organization, under Manage , select Password reset .

Question 2 (Case 1, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Configure SSPR'?

A. In your Microsoft Entra organization, under Manage , select Password reset .
B. Any user who is signed in can change their password, regardless of the edition of Microsoft Entra ID.
C. In a hybrid situation, where you have Active Directory on-premises and Microsoft Entra ID in the cloud, any password change in the cloud must be written back to the on-premises directory.
D. Go to the Azure portal , then to Microsoft Entra ID > Manage > Password reset .

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Configure SSPR' in this chapter.
> Go to the Azure portal , then to Microsoft Entra ID > Manage > Password reset .

Question 3 (Case 1, Q3)

B. All : All users in the Microsoft Entra organization can use SSPR.

D. Turn off self-service password reset : Go to Microsoft Entra ID > Manage > Password reset . Under Self service password reset enabled , select None . Select Save .

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Authenticate a password reset' in this chapter.
> Azure supports six different ways to authenticate reset requests.

Correct answer: B

Chapter 9: Module 3.1: Configure virtual networks

Case Study 1

Current state: Suppose addresses 10.0.0.4 through 10.0.0.9 are already assigned to other resources.

Operational detail: A private IP address is allocated from the address range of the virtual network subnet that a resource is deployed in.

Constraint: Your company is looking to migrate their on-premises infrastructure to Azure.

A. An Azure virtual network is a logical isolation of the Azure cloud resources.
B. Your first task is to put in place the virtual networks and subnets.
C. Copilot can assist you in designing Azure infrastructure solutions.
D. Your company is migrating to Azure and replicating their on-premises network in the cloud.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Things to know about Azure virtual networks' in this chapter.
> An Azure virtual network is a logical isolation of the Azure cloud resources.

D. Azure virtual networks are an essential component for creating private networks in Azure.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Introduction' in this chapter.
> Azure virtual networks are an essential component for creating private networks in Azure.

C. Copilot can assist you in designing Azure infrastructure solutions.
D. You're exploring how to use default rules to apply security to inbound traffic from virtual machines within your virtual network.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Things to consider when creating effective rules' in this chapter.
> When you create a network security group, you must define an allow rule for both the subnet and network interface in the group to ensure traffic can get through.

Chapter 11: Module 3.3: Host your domain on Azure DNS

Subtitle: What is Azure DNS? / What is DNS? / How does DNS work?

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'What is Azure DNS? / What is DNS? / How does DNS work?'.

Current state: In order for a computer, server, or other network-enabled device to access web-based resources, it must reference a DNS server.

Operational detail: An alias record allows you to link the zone apex (wideworldimports.com) to a load balancer.

Constraint: DNS zones are supported as part of the Azure infrastructure, so there's no need to invest in a DNS solution.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 3.3: Host your domain on Azure DNS' guidance.

> Select Create > DNS zone . The Create DNS zone pane appears.

Case Study 5

Scenario: During a governance review for 'What is Azure DNS? / What is DNS? / How does DNS work?', you must provide a defensible recommendation.

Current state: When you connect by using your on-premises network, the DNS settings come from your server.

Operational detail: If the domain isn't in the cache, it contacts one or more DNS servers on the web to see if they have a match. When a match is found, the DNS server updates the local cache and resolves the request.

Constraint: If you don't have an Azure account or you don't want to create one at this time, you can read through the instructions so you understand the information that's being presented.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 3.3: Host your domain on Azure DNS' guidance.

Question 21 (Case 5, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Private domains'?

A. Unit 3.3.6: Exercise - Create alias records for Azure DNS
B. In the optional exercises for this module, you created resources by using your own Azure subscription.
C. If you don't have an Azure account or you don't want to create one at this time, you can read through the instructions so you understand the information that's being presented.
D. To publish a private DNS zone to your virtual network, you specify the list of virtual networks that are allowed to resolve records within the zone.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Private domains' in this chapter.
> To publish a private DNS zone to your virtual network, you specify the list of virtual networks that are allowed to resolve records within the zone.

Question 22 (Case 5, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Create a DNS zone in Azure DNS'?

A. To create the private zone, you need enter a resource group and the name of the zone.
B. When deployment is complete, select Go to resource .
C. As the administrator for your company, you want to set up a DNS server by using Azure DNS.
D. Maintains the key-value pair database of IP addresses and any host or subdomain over which the DNS server has authority. This function is often associated with mail, web, and other internet domain services.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Create a DNS zone in Azure DNS' in this chapter.
> When deployment is complete, select Go to resource .

Question 23 (Case 5, Q3)
Which response matches the recommended approach in 'Domain lookup requests'?

A. Unit 3.3.5: Dynamically resolve resource name by using alias record
B. Quickstart: Create an Azure private DNS zone by using the Azure portal
C. You can't use Azure DNS to register a domain name; you need to register it by using a third-party domain registrar.
D. If the domain name is stored in the short-term cache, the DNS server resolves the domain request.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Domain lookup requests' in this chapter.
> If the domain name is stored in the short-term cache, the DNS server resolves the domain request.

Question 24 (Case 5, Q4)
Which statement best fits the requirement described in 'DNS server assignment'?

A. When you connect by using an external location like a hotel, the DNS settings come from the internet service provider (ISP).
B. By using an Azure DNS zone to host your domain, your organization benefits by having all the resources managed through a single, common interface.
C. Learn how to configure an Azure alias at the zone apex to direct to the load balancer.
D. You now know you can use an Azure alias record to provide a dynamic, automatically refreshing link between the zone apex and the load balancer.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'DNS server assignment' in this chapter.
> When you connect by using an external location like a hotel, the DNS settings come from the internet service provider (ISP).

Question 25 (Case 5, Q5)
Which action best aligns with the guidance in section 'Step 3: Update the domain registrar setting'?

A. TXT is the text record. It's used to associate text strings with a domain name. Azure and Microsoft 365 use TXT records to verify domain ownership.
B. A is the host record, and is the most common type of DNS record. It maps the domain or host name to the IP address.
C. When you delegate the domain, you must use all four name servers provided by Azure DNS.
D. When deployment is complete, select Go to resource . The Overview pane for your DNS zone appears.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Step 3: Update the domain registrar setting' in this chapter.
> When you delegate the domain, you must use all four name servers provided by Azure DNS.

Case Study 6

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'What is Azure DNS? / What is DNS? / How does DNS work?'.

Current state: To publish a private DNS zone to your virtual network, you specify the list of virtual networks that are allowed to resolve records within the zone.

Operational detail: You can use it throughout your on-premises network.

Constraint: Configuration information for your DNS server is stored as a file within a zone on your DNS server.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 3.3: Host your domain on Azure DNS' guidance.

Question 26 (Case 6, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Verify your global Azure DNS'?

A. From the dropdown list, select the resource group you want to use for this exercise.
B. You also learn about Azure DNS and why you'd use it.
C. An alias record allows you to link the zone apex (wideworldimports.com) to a load balancer.
D. In a real-world scenario, after you create the public DNS zone, you update the NS records of the domain-name registrar to delegate the domain to Azure.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Verify your global Azure DNS' in this chapter.
> In a real-world scenario, after you create the public DNS zone, you update the NS records of the domain-name registrar to delegate the domain to Azure.

Question 27 (Case 6, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Private domains'?

A. Create and configure a DNS zone for your domain by using Azure DNS.
B. Split-horizon DNS support allows the same domain name to exist in both private and public zones. It resolves to the correct one based on the originating request location.
C. Many network devices are now provisioned with both an IPv4 and an IPv6 address.
D. An alias record allows you to link the zone apex (wideworldimports.com) to a load balancer.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Private domains' in this chapter.
> Split-horizon DNS support allows the same domain name to exist in both private and public zones. It resolves to the correct one based on the originating request location.

D. Many network devices are now provisioned with both an IPv4 and an IPv6 address.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Private domains' in this chapter.
> DNS zones are supported as part of the Azure infrastructure, so there's no need to invest in a DNS solution.

Correct answer: B

Question 65 (Case 13, Q5)

Chapter 14: Module 3.6: Introduction to Azure Load Balancer

Subtitle: What is Azure Load Balancer? / How Azure Load Balancer works / Front-end IP

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'What is Azure Load Balancer? / How Azure Load Balancer works / Front-end IP'.

Current state: This rule combination allows remote desktop access from outside of Azure.

Operational detail: Azure load balancers can have multiple front-end IPs.

Constraint: It also includes using session persistence to ensure that clients only communicate with a single VM during a session.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 3.6: Introduction to Azure Load Balancer' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Session persistence'?

A. This rule combination allows remote desktop access from outside of Azure.
B. The back-end pool is a group of VMs or instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set that responds to the incoming request.
C. Front-end IP addresses and virtual networks are never directly exposed to an internet endpoint.
D. None (default) : Specifies that any healthy VM can handle the request.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Session persistence' in this chapter.
> None (default) : Specifies that any healthy VM can handle the request.

Question 2 (Case 1, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'When to use Azure Load Balancer'?

A. These VMs are hosted across various virtual networks.
B. An Azure service that allows you to evenly distribute incoming network traffic across a group of Azure virtual machines, or across instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set.
C. Azure Load Balancer is best suited for applications that require ultra-low latency and high performance.
D. Now that the VMs are migrated to Azure, you would like to replicate the functionality provided by the Layer 4 hardware devices using native Azure services.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'When to use Azure Load Balancer' in this chapter.
> Azure Load Balancer is best suited for applications that require ultra-low latency and high performance.

D. You can distribute specific types of traffic across multiple VMs or services by applying load-balancing rules.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Front-end IP' in this chapter.
> You can distribute specific types of traffic across multiple VMs or services by applying load-balancing rules.

Question 4 (Case 1, Q4)

B. Load Balancer allows you to configure different health probe types for endpoints: TCP, HTTP, and HTTPS.

D. For example, you could use NAT from the load balancer's public address to TCP 3389 on a specific VM.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Session persistence' in this chapter.
> The default behavior (None) is that any healthy VM can handle successive requests from a client.

Question 5 (Case 1, Q5)
Which action best aligns with the guidance in section 'Summary'?

A. An Azure service that allows you to evenly distribute incoming network traffic across a group of Azure virtual machines, or across instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set.
B. Health probes ensure the resources in the back end are healthy and that traffic isn't directed to unhealthy back-end instances.
C. The back-end pool is a group of VMs or instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set that responds to the incoming request.
D. Instead of continuously adding network capacity, processors, disk resources, and RAM, you can address this traffic by implementing load balancing.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Summary' in this chapter.
> An Azure service that allows you to evenly distribute incoming network traffic across a group of Azure virtual machines, or across instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set.

> Azure load balancers can have multiple front-end IPs.

Which statement best fits the requirement described in 'What is Azure Load Balancer?'?

A. You can configure public load balancers for front-end traffic to web tiers of applications.
B. The back-end pool can be made up of Azure infrastructure as a service (IaaS) VMs or instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set.
C. Azure Load Balancer is an Azure service that allows you to evenly distribute incoming network traffic across a group of Azure VMs, or across instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set.
D. The back-end pool is a group of VMs or instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set that responds to the incoming request.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'What is Azure Load Balancer?' in this chapter.
> Azure Load Balancer is an Azure service that allows you to evenly distribute incoming network traffic across a group of Azure VMs, or across instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set.

> The back-end pool is a group of VMs or instances in a Virtual Machine Scale Set that responds to the incoming request.

Chapter 15: Module 3.7: Introduction to Azure Application Gateway

Subtitle: What is Azure Application Gateway? / How Azure Application Gateway works / Load balancing in Application Gateway

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'What is Azure Application Gateway? / How Azure Application Gateway works / Load balancing in Application Gateway'.

Current state: If you're using Azure App Service to host the back-end application, you don't need to install any certificates in Application Gateway to connect to the back-end pool.

Operational detail: At the moment, a special but aging hardware device is deployed on the Adatum perimeter network that manages traffic to the web applications hosted on these computers.

Constraint: You learned that Application Gateway provides features such as load balancing HTTP traffic, a web application firewall, and support for TLS/SSL encryption of your data.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 3.7: Introduction to Azure Application Gateway' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Multiple-site routing'?

A. These protocols have low overhead (unlike HTTP) and can reuse the same TCP connection for multiple request/responses resulting in a more efficient resource utilization.
B. You can open many ports, and Application Gateway can receive messages on any of these ports.
C. Azure Application Gateway manages the requests that client applications send to web apps that are hosted on a pool of web servers.
D. Multi-site configurations are useful for supporting multitenant applications, where each tenant has its own set of virtual machines or other resources hosting a web application.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Multiple-site routing' in this chapter.
> Multi-site configurations are useful for supporting multitenant applications, where each tenant has its own set of virtual machines or other resources hosting a web application.

Question 2 (Case 1, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'What is Azure Application Gateway?'?

A. You can open many ports, and Application Gateway can receive messages on any of these ports.
B. Azure Application Gateway is an Azure service that processes traffic to web apps that are hosted on a pool of web servers.
C. Application Gateway provides features such as load balancing HTTP traffic and web application firewall.
D. At the moment, a special but aging hardware device is deployed on the Adatum perimeter network that manages traffic to the web applications hosted on these computers.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'What is Azure Application Gateway?' in this chapter.
> Application Gateway provides features such as load balancing HTTP traffic and web application firewall.

Question 3 (Case 1, Q3)
Which response matches the recommended approach in 'When to use Azure Application Gateway'?

A. Azure Application Gateway allows Adatum to use a web application firewall to block cross-site scripting and SQL injection traffic before it reaches servers in the back-end pool.
B. If you don't configure a health probe, Application Gateway creates a default probe that waits for 30 seconds before deciding that a server is unavailable.
C. Session stickiness is especially important with e-commerce applications where you don’t want a transaction to be disrupted because the load balancer bounces it around between back-end servers.
D. Learn what Azure Application Gateway is and the functionality it provides.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'When to use Azure Application Gateway' in this chapter.
> Azure Application Gateway allows Adatum to use a web application firewall to block cross-site scripting and SQL injection traffic before it reaches servers in the back-end pool.

Question 4 (Case 1, Q4)
Which statement best fits the requirement described in 'WebSocket and HTTP/2 traffic'?

A. Custom error pages . Application Gateway allows you to create custom error pages instead of displaying default error pages. You can use your own branding and layout using a custom error page.
B. You learned that Application Gateway provides features such as load balancing HTTP traffic, a web application firewall, and support for TLS/SSL encryption of your data.
C. These protocols have low overhead (unlike HTTP) and can reuse the same TCP connection for multiple request/responses resulting in a more efficient resource utilization.
D. Azure provides other load balancing solutions, including Azure Front Door, Azure Traffic Manager, and Azure Load Balancer.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'WebSocket and HTTP/2 traffic' in this chapter.
> These protocols have low overhead (unlike HTTP) and can reuse the same TCP connection for multiple request/responses resulting in a more efficient resource utilization.

Question 5 (Case 1, Q5)
Which action best aligns with the guidance in section 'WebSocket and HTTP/2 traffic'?

A. Application Gateway supports autoscaling, and can scale up or down based on changing traffic load patterns.
B. If you don't configure a health probe, Application Gateway creates a default probe that waits for 30 seconds before deciding that a server is unavailable.
C. Application Gateway provides native support for the WebSocket and HTTP/2 protocols.
D. A back-end pool is a collection of web servers that can be made up of: a fixed set of virtual machines, a virtual machine scale-set, an app hosted by Azure App Services, or a collection of on-premises servers.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'WebSocket and HTTP/2 traffic' in this chapter.
> Application Gateway provides native support for the WebSocket and HTTP/2 protocols.

A. The goal of this module is to provide Azure Administrators with the knowledge and skills to effectively configure and manage Azure storage accounts.
B. All storage account types are encrypted by using Storage Service Encryption (SSE) for data at rest.
C. We create the URL to access an object in your storage account by appending the object's location in the storage account to the endpoint.
D. Consider secure access . Azure Storage encrypts all data. Azure Storage provides you with fine-grained control over who has access to your data.

Correct answer: A
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Introduction' in this chapter.
> The goal of this module is to provide Azure Administrators with the knowledge and skills to effectively configure and manage Azure storage accounts.

Question 62 (Case 13, Q2)

> You can use Standard storage for applications that require bulk storage or where data is infrequently accessed.

C. By designing policy rules to adjust storage tiers in respect to the age of data, you can design the least expensive storage options for your needs.
D. Use the Then clause to set the transition action for the blob data.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Things to know about blob object replication' in this chapter.
> Object replication requires that Blob versioning is enabled on both the source and destination accounts.

Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Recommendations for managing risks' in this chapter.
> In some scenarios, the risks associated with a particular operation against your Azure storage account outweigh the benefits of using a SAS.

> If you want to make all blobs in a container publicly readable, you can make the container Public, rather than providing a SAS to every client for access.

> Consider application lift and shift . Use Azure File Sync to move applications that require access between Azure and on-premises systems. Provide write access to the same data across Windows Servers and Azure Files.

Constraint: When you implement Virtual Machine Scale Sets and configure all your virtual machines in the same way, you gain true autoscaling .

Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Things to know about fault domains' in this chapter.

Correct answer: C

> An Azure Virtual Machine Scale Sets implementation can automatically increase or decrease the number of virtual machine instances that run your application.

Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Learn more with documentation' in this chapter.
> Azure App Service plans . This article provides an overview of App Service plans.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Things to know about container groups' in this chapter.
> You must expose the port on the IP address and from the container to enable external clients to reach a container in your group.

Chapter 26: Module 6.1: Introduction to Azure Backup

Subtitle: Example scenario / What will we be doing? / What is the main goal?

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'Example scenario / What will we be doing? / What is the main goal?'.

Current state: Azure Backup uses Recovery Services vaults and Backup vaults to orchestrate and manage backups.

Operational detail: Azure Backup compliance standards.

Constraint: A vault is an online-storage entity in Azure that's used to hold data such as backup copies, recovery points, and backup policies.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 6.1: Introduction to Azure Backup' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Does Azure Backup help with compliance?'?

A. It increases ease of use by automating storage management.
B. It's also an Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC) boundary, providing you with the option to restrict access to backups only to authorized Backup Admins.
C. Vault-standard tier : Backup data for all workloads supported by Azure Backup is stored in vaults, which hold backup storage, an autoscaling set of storage accounts managed by Azure Backup.
D. Backup center allows you to have a single pane of glass to manage all tasks related to backups.

Correct answer: B
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Does Azure Backup help with compliance?' in this chapter.
> It's also an Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC) boundary, providing you with the option to restrict access to backups only to authorized Backup Admins.

Question 2 (Case 1, Q2)
Which option should the administrator choose based on 'Workload integration layer - Backup Extension'?

A. This container ensures that restores are quick, because the backup/snapshot is available locally to the customer.
B. You can then back that server up to a Recovery Services vault in Azure.
C. You can use a single vault or multiple vaults to organize and manage your backup.
D. The backup data is eventually transferred to Azure Backup managed storage in the data plane by using secure Azure networks Network Security Groups (NSG), Firewalls, or more sophisticated private endpoints.

Correct answer: D
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Workload integration layer - Backup Extension' in this chapter.
> The backup data is eventually transferred to Azure Backup managed storage in the data plane by using secure Azure networks Network Security Groups (NSG), Firewalls, or more sophisticated private endpoints.

C. Azure Backup provides in-built job monitoring for operations such as configuring backup, backing up, restoring, deleting backups, and so on.

Correct answer: C
Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Does Azure Backup simplify monitoring and administration?' in this chapter.

> Vaults (Recovery Services and Backup vaults) provide the management capabilities and are accessible via the Azure portal, Backup Center, Vault dashboards, SDK, CLI, and even REST APIs.

Chapter 27: Module 6.2: Protect your virtual machines by using Azure Backup

Subtitle: Azure Backup features and scenarios / What is Azure Backup? / Back up an Azure virtual machine by using Azure Backup

Case Study 1

Scenario: Your organization is implementing Azure changes in the area of 'Azure Backup features and scenarios / What is Azure Backup? / Back up an Azure virtual machine by using Azure Backup'.

Current state: Encryption of backups : Backup data is automatically encrypted using Microsoft-managed keys. Alternatively, you can encrypt your backed-up data using customer-managed keys stored in the Azure Key Vault.

Operational detail: Existing disks connected to the VM are replaced with the selected restore point.

Constraint: The Backup Jobs pane appears.

Objective: Choose the best answers for the linked questions in this case, based on chapter 'Module 6.2: Protect your virtual machines by using Azure Backup' guidance.

Question 1 (Case 1, Q1)
Which choice is most consistent with the chapter guidance for 'Azure Backup features and scenarios'?

A. The new VM must be created in the same region as the source VM.

Explanation: The correct option matches a source statement under section label 'Azure Backup versus Azure Site Recovery' in this chapter.
> In that sense, if there are issues like network or power outages, you can use availability zones.
